ops.text: Phase 4 watcher — reported "stall" INVESTIGATED, premise FALSIFIED (no bug). The alarm (PID 5980 spinning, "no proposals ~24h") was not a stall: shadow.db had already advanced to 14 rows (latest 2026-07-12T03:44:23Z; rows 13/14 = sessions 963eb8f5/4eec2e93, fired minutes before the look). PID 5980 was running the FIXED code (f3ffe31 synchronous draft), not stale pre-fix: the install is editable so it imports from source; drafter_mlx.py mtime 10:15, process start 10:16:05 (10s after the fix saved); daemon.run() has no try/except, so the buggy worker-thread path (RuntimeError "no Stream(cpu,0)") would kill the loop on the first draft — 14 successful drafts prove fixed code. The to_thread traceback in watch.log is from an earlier pre-fix invocation that exited. The 22h proposal gap (row 12 Jul10 22:17 PDT -> row 13 Jul11 20:31 PDT) = benign gate selectivity: that window's activity was mostly subagent/workflow SIDECHAINS (dropped by ingest, daemon.py:103) plus main sessions already proposed (rows 1-8) under cooldown_turns:5 / max_per_session:5 caps; `sample 5980 5` showed 59% kevent-idle + ~14% transcript parse, no MLX/worker frames (a healthy poll loop, not a spin or wedge). ACTIONS: (1) closed f3ffe31's acknowledged test blind spot ("the sync parity test never crossed a thread") with a thread-affinity regression test asserting draft() runs decide() on the caller thread — commit 19ea7e4 test(watcher); (2) moved the watcher under launchd supervision — new com.cc-steer.watch KeepAlive+RunAtLoad agent rendered by cc_steer/launchd.py (render_watch/watch_command/install_watch/uninstall_watch), wired into `cc-steer pipeline install-launchd --watch`, with test_launchd.py cases — commit be35600 feat(launchd). Killed the unsupervised 5965/5980 (kill-old-before-bootstrap under E8 8B-serve memory pressure), bootstrapped the agent; the watcher reloaded MLX cleanly (peak ~3.2GB resident, mmap weights page out under pressure, no OOM/thrash) and re-entered its loop; KeepAlive PROVEN — killed the child (SIGTERM) and launchd respawned in <3s with a new PID that reloaded MLX and re-entered the loop. Both commits on main (be35600, fast-forward 42a2e8e -> be35600); 251 pytest green locally, Linux `ty` green in CI. CI pytest is RED but PRE-EXISTING and unrelated to this change: ci.yml clones cc-transcript HEAD (unreleased v10), which dropped cc_transcript.mining.sample_windows and the mining.sampling module, so test_cli_sync/daemon/negatives/pipeline/shadow fail at COLLECTION on every recent main commit (42a2e8e, f3ffe31, ...) — a separate cc-transcript-v10 migration, not a regression here. The watcher daemon itself does not use mining (imports cc_transcript.watch/activity). Restart or model promotion now = launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.cc-steer.watch. Open verification item at handoff: shadow.db still at 14 post-restart — a new row needs the lexical gate (0.5) to fire on a fresh eligible turn during live activity (gate-timing, not a fault).
